### ✅ ITEM 7: Frontend Infrastructure Setup (React 18 + Vite)

**Status**: COMPLETED  
**Goal**: Create ALL foundational React 18 + Vite files required before any page can run. This item MUST be completed first and completely. Includes: package.json, framework config with /api/* proxy, entrypoint, router / app shell with routes for all pages, base HTTP client (RELATIVE URLs ONLY), auth state management, nginx.conf (proxy /api/* → backend), multi-stage Dockerfile, and frontend service in docker-compose.yml. READ these context files before implementing: `docs/uiux/design-contract.md`, `docs/uiux/api-contract.yaml`, `docs/uiux/component-guide.md`.

**Files Created/Modified**:
- ✅ `frontend/package.json` (create) - react@18, react-dom@18, react-router-dom@6, @tanstack/react-query@5, axios. DevDeps: vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, typescript, @types/react, @playwright/test. Scripts: dev, build, preview, test:e2e.
- ✅ `frontend/vite.config.ts` (create) - plugins=[react()], server.proxy={ '/api': { target: 'http://backend:23001', changeOrigin: true } }. NO define block with API URLs.
- ✅ `frontend/tsconfig.json` (create) - TypeScript strict config, jsx: react-jsx, paths alias @/* → src/*.
- ✅ `frontend/index.html` (create) - <div id='root'> + <script type='module' src='/src/main.tsx'>
- ✅ `frontend/src/main.tsx` (create) - Bootstrap: ReactDOM.createRoot.render(<QueryClientProvider><BrowserRouter><AuthProvider><App /></AuthProvider></BrowserRouter></QueryClientProvider>)
- ✅ `frontend/src/App.tsx` (create) - <Routes> with one <Route> per page: /iniciohome, /catlogodeproductos, /detalledeproducto, /carritodecompras, /login, /register, /productlist, /productdetail, /orderhistory, /orderdetail. Catch-all → NotFoundPage. Protected routes wrapped in <RequireAuth>.
- ✅ `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` (create) - Base HTTP client. ONLY relative URLs. Reads JWT from localStorage('token'). Attaches Authorization: Bearer. Throws ApiError(message, status) on non-2xx.
- ✅ `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` (create) - React context: { user, token, login(), logout() }. Exports useAuth().
- ✅ `frontend/nginx.conf` (create) - nginx: root /usr/share/nginx/html; try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html (SPA). location /api/ { proxy_pass http://backend:23001/api/; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; } listen 80.
- ✅ `frontend/Dockerfile` (create) - Stage 1 (node:20-alpine AS builder): npm ci + npm run build. Stage 2 (nginx:alpine): COPY dist → /usr/share/nginx/html + nginx.conf. EXPOSE 80.
- ✅ `docker-compose.yml` (modify) - Add 'frontend' service: build: { context: ./frontend }, ports: ['3000:80'], depends_on: [backend], network: project_network. NO API_URL env vars in the environment block.
- ✅ `frontend/package-lock.json` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/CartPage.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/.gitkeep` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetailPage.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/e2e/products.spec.ts` (created)

### ✅ ITEM 16: Frontend E2E Tests — Full-Stack Integration

**Status**: COMPLETED  
**Goal**: Write Playwright E2E tests that drive real browser UI events and verify correct backend API responses. RULES: (1) Each test navigates to a real page. (2) Uses page.click()/fill()/select() to trigger actions. (3) Uses page.waitForResponse() to intercept and assert the correct API endpoint was called with correct payload. (4) Asserts backend response data appears in DOM. NO MOCKS — tests hit the real backend via docker-compose. API contract at `docs/uiux/api-contract.yaml`.

**Files Created/Modified**:
- ✅ `frontend/e2e/auth.spec.ts` (create) - Register new user → intercept POST /api/auth/register, assert 201. Login → intercept POST /api/auth/login, assert token stored in localStorage. Access protected route → assert Authorization header sent. Logout → assert localStorage cleared.
- ✅ `frontend/e2e/products.spec.ts` (create) - Load product list → waitForResponse(/\/api\/products/), assert items rendered. Click product card → waitForResponse(/\/api\/products\/.+/), assert detail shown. Search/filter if UI has it → assert query params sent.
- ✅ `frontend/e2e/checkout.spec.ts` (create) - Full checkout flow: add item → go to cart → click checkout → waitForResponse(/\/api\/orders/), assert POST body has cart items. Order history → waitForResponse GET /api/orders, assert list rendered. Order detail → assert GET /api/orders/:id data shown.
- ✅ `frontend/playwright.config.ts` (create) - Playwright config: baseURL='http://localhost:3000', testDir='./e2e', timeout=30000. Reference docs/uiux/api-contract.yaml for endpoint paths. No webServer block — assume docker-compose up is run externally.
- ✅ `backend/auth-service/test/auth.e2e-spec.ts` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/package-lock.json` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/CartPage.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/tsconfig.json` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/.gitkeep` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetailPage.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/Dockerfile` (created)
- ✅ `backend/user-service/run_tests.sh` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/App.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx` (created)
- ✅ `backend/auth-service/run_tests.sh` (created)
- ✅ `backend/product-service/run_tests.sh` (created)
- ✅ `frontend/tsconfig.node.json` (created)

## Original Development Plan

<details>
<summary>Click to expand full development plan</summary>

```markdown
# DEVELOPMENT PLAN: Project

## 1. ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW

**Architecture:**  
- Microservices (NestJS/Node.js 20, TypeScript 5)
- PostgreSQL 15 per service (RDS in prod)
- Redis 7 for caching/session (ElastiCache in prod)
- RabbitMQ 3.x for async messaging
- AWS S3 for product images (integration stubbed for MVP)
- Dockerized, orchestrated with docker-compose (local) and Kubernetes (EKS in prod)
- API Gateway (future), direct REST for MVP

**Services:**  
- **auth-service** (port 23001): User registration, login, JWT, refresh, user info
- **product-service** (port 23002): Product CRUD, category filtering
- **order-service** (port 23003): Order creation, status, history
- **user-service** (port 23004): User profile, admin user management

**Shared:**  
- DTOs/interfaces for Product, User, Order, Auth
- JWT utilities
- Shared config/constants

**Database:**  
- Each service has its own DB schema (PostgreSQL)
- Models: User, Product, Order, OrderItem, Category, Payment, Notification

**Infrastructure:**  
- docker-compose.yml for all services, Redis, RabbitMQ, Postgres
- .env.example for all required variables
- run.sh for local orchestration
- README.md for setup

**Testing:**  
- Jest for backend unit/integration tests
- E2E test per service

**Folder Structure:**  
- backend/auth-service/
- backend/product-service/
- backend/order-service/
- backend/user-service/
- backend/shared/
- frontend/ (not in scope for backend-only phase)
- docker-compose.yml, .env.example, run.sh, README.md at root

## 2. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

1. All backend services start via `./run.sh`, pass healthchecks, and expose their documented REST endpoints.
2. Each service auto-initializes its database schema and seeds with 3–5 example records on first run.
3. All endpoints validate input, enforce RBAC (where required), and return correct responses as per SPEC.md.
4. JWT authentication and role-based access are enforced on all protected endpoints.
5. All services log in structured JSON format and fail fast if required environment variables are missing.
6. E2E tests for each service pass: happy path and error cases for each endpoint.
7. Infrastructure scripts (.env.example, run.sh, docker-compose.yml) enable zero-manual-steps local setup.

## TEAM SCOPE (MANDATORY — PARSED BY THE PIPELINE)
Every executable item MUST include exactly one line at the end of the item block (after Validation):
**Role:** <role_id> (<category>)

---

## 3. EXECUTABLE ITEMS

### ITEM 1: Foundation — shared types, DTOs, DB schemas, config, utilities
**Goal:**  
Establish all shared code and contracts for the backend microservices.  
Includes:  
- DTOs/interfaces for Product, User, Order, Auth (as per SPEC.md)
- JWT utility functions
- Shared config (env validation, constants)
- Database entity definitions for User, Product, Order, OrderItem, Category, Payment, Notification (TypeORM entities)
- Shared error classes

**Files to create:**
- backend/shared/dto/product.dto.ts
- backend/shared/dto/user.dto.ts
- backend/shared/dto/order.dto.ts
- backend/shared/dto/auth.dto.ts
- backend/shared/utils/jwt.ts
- backend/shared/config/env.ts
- backend/shared/entities/product.entity.ts
- backend/shared/entities/user.entity.ts
- backend/shared/entities/order.entity.ts
- backend/shared/entities/category.entity.ts
- backend/shared/entities/payment.entity.ts
- backend/shared/entities/notification.entity.ts

**Tests required:**
- backend/shared/tests/jwt.util.test.ts (JWT sign/verify/expiration)
- backend/shared/tests/env.test.ts (env validation, missing/invalid vars)
- backend/shared/tests/entities.test.ts (entity instantiation, relations)

**Dependencies:** None

- `npm run test` in backend/shared passes all tests

**Role:** role-tl (technical_lead)

### ITEM 2: Auth Service — registration, login, JWT, refresh, user info
**Goal:**  
Implement the authentication service with endpoints:  
- POST /api/auth/register (register new user)
- POST /api/auth/login (login, issue JWT/refresh)
- POST /api/auth/refresh (refresh tokens)
- GET /api/auth/me (current user info, JWT required)

- backend/auth-service/src/auth.controller.ts (all endpoints)

- backend/auth-service/src/user.entity.ts (import from shared)

- backend/auth-service/package.json

- backend/auth-service/nest-cli.json
```
